element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational movement established in first levels
Introduced early, becomes core traversal mechanic throughout game
Targeting system learned through gentle introduction, refined across levels
Expanded from Super Mario World predecessor

element_introduction_order.element: Baby Mario timer pressure (fall into bubble)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early levels after initial safe introduction
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core risk mechanic; player learns to manage timer by collecting stars
Used to break soft earth and bury objects

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Various levels (World 1-4 onward based on level design notes)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each transformation introduces unique mechanics; staggered introduction prevents cognitive overload
Power-up variant; extends egg mechanic concept
Provides temporary invulnerability and speed boost

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Every 4th level (2 per world, first at World 1-4)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Escalating difficulty; pattern recognition against specific enemy types
Optional exploration encouraged; reward structure incentivizes thorough level exploration
Environmental awareness becomes critical; no timer management possible when hit
Integral to level design; often requires eggs or transformations

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting stars to reset Baby Mario timer
typical_tension_triggers: Baby Mario falling into bubble (timer begins countdown)
Puzzle sections requiring specific egg/transformation combo
Moving platform sequences with tight timing
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating tension-relief cycles with escalating difficulty curve. Early worlds use frequent relief checkpoints; later worlds (4-6) extend tension sequences and reduce safe zones. Boss fights represent major tension peaks every 4 levels.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden collectibles (red coins, flowers) marked by visual design cues; secret paths often in corners or behind breakable walls; off-screen areas require exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Walls that appear missing textures indicate breakable sections or hidden areas (design notes reference 'wall ledge that does not appear'). Egg targets hint at destructible environmental elements. Elevated or isolated platforms suggest optional paths.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle tutorial pacing. World 1 introduces core mechanics (flutter jump, eggs, timer management) in safe context with frequent relief opportunities. Linear progression with generous platform spacing.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Worlds 2-3 escalate puzzle complexity and hazard density. Introduction of timed sequences and more aggressive enemy placement. Transformation mechanics expand repertoire. Boss difficulty increases.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Worlds 4-6 compress safe zones, extend tension sequences, and combine multiple mechanics simultaneously. 'KEEP MOVING!!!!' and 'The Very Loooooong Cave' exemplify sustained pressure design. Final boss requires mastery of all mechanics.
notable_spikes: Transition from World 1 to World 2 (puzzle complexity jump)
'The Impossible? Maze' (World 5 design notes indicate maze-based challenge)

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear progression with frequent platforms, minimal instant-death hazards, generous safe zones. Introduces one mechanic per section. Heavy use of gentle slopes and breakable obstacles that reward egg use.
Moving platforms increase in speed and number. Enemy placement becomes pattern-based. Instant-death hazards cluster in critical path areas. Timer pressure becomes secondary to environmental skill.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Watch Out Below!, Shifting Platforms Ahead

notable_level_patterns.description: Level designed around a single transformation (car, drill, submarine, helicopter). Environment tailored to transformation capabilities. Puzzle-solving through transformation mechanic exploitation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Levels containing car/train sections, underwater segments

notable_level_patterns.description: Multiple optional paths with dead-ends; collectible gathering requires full exploration. Rewards thorough players with hidden items and higher scores.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: The Cave of the Mystery Maze, The Impossible? Maze

notable_level_patterns.description: Screen scrolls automatically or level auto-advances, forcing constant forward momentum. No time for hesitation. Hazards aligned with scroll speed for constant threat.
Large variant of enemy type encountered earlier. Pattern-based attack telegraphing. Requires egg aiming precision and positioning. Represents skill checkpoint of accumulated knowledge.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: World X-4 (Burt the Bashful's Fort, Salvo the Slime's Castle, etc.)

notable_level_patterns.description: Level sections with branching paths that converge; collectible placement rewards revisiting sections. Off-screen elements may be accessible only after acquiring specific items.
